mechanism_narrative: A persistently near-zero federal funds rate compresses the equity discount rate and incentivizes risk-asset allocation over cash, producing a positive drift in S&P 500 returns as long as FEDFUNDS remains below 1% with no upward trend.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or SPX posts negative median 20d return while FEDFUNDS stays below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals accelerating labor market repair after a shock, boosting consumer income expectations and corporate earnings outlooks, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns once UNRATE falls at least 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=6.0 as of 2021-03 declining from pandemic peak
failure_condition: In triggered instances, forward 20d SPX return is negative or not statistically distinguishable from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion that is not decomposable into either series alone; the joint acceleration of RSAFS and HOUST predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter because it signals durable, multi-sector demand momentum rather than a single-sector blip.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth > 2% AND HOUST 3-month growth > 5% simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=619105 and HOUST=1421 as of early 2021 both elevated post-stimulus
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances show 60d forward SPX return not significantly greater than instances where only one series is elevated (difference-in-means p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock industrial recovery, sustained month-over-month increases in industrial production signal restoring supply-side capacity and corporate revenue normalization, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over a full quarter specifically while the economy remains in the early expansion phase following a recession.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=105.583 as of 2021-03 rising from 2020 trough
failure_condition: Hypothesis is rejected if the positive INDPRO-return relationship reverses sign or Rank IC < 0.03 once the recovery phase matures into a late-cycle regime, tested via rolling 3-year windows split by NBER-defined expansion age.

mechanism_narrative: When federal funds rate stays near zero while CPI inflation accelerates, the combination signals a negative real interest rate environment that initially boosts equity valuations via financial repression, but the joint condition of rising CPI with unchanged FEDFUNDS is a leading indicator of future policy tightening risk, producing a nonlinear interaction effect on forward returns that single-series models miss.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with rising inflation
failure_condition: If triggered instances produce negative median 20d SPX return or the interaction term's marginal contribution to Rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from the sum of individual FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL effects (F-test p > 0.10), hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects real-time labor demand strength that is quickly priced into equities; a positive PAYEMS trend produces a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next trading week as markets react to payroll momentum.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=144120 thousand as of 2021-03 recovering from pandemic job losses
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly change and forward 5d SPX return falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or sign of correlation flips persistently for more than 6 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp surge in housing starts above a historical threshold signals overheating consumer demand and forward-looking construction activity that pulls equity gains forward, but such surges historically precede short-term equity pullbacks as markets price in tightening expectations within the following month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST level exceeds 1500 thousand units (annualized) or rises > 10% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=1421 as of 2021-02 near multi-year highs
failure_condition: If SPX forward 5d return is positive in more than 55% of triggered instances across a 2-year rolling sample, or Rank IC sign is positive, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary regime of near-zero rates, an accelerating industrial production recovery combined with rising CPI signals reflationary supply-side normalization that boosts cyclical equity returns; but this relationship is regime-specific and inverts once inflation becomes a policy concern, so it is expected to hold only in the early reflation phase of a recovery.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=105.583 rising with FEDFUNDS=0.07 as of 2021-03
regime_hint: early reflation recovery under zero-rate policy
failure_condition: Hypothesis is rejected if forward 60d SPX returns in triggered periods are not significantly different from non-triggered periods (p > 0.05), or if the relationship's direction flips once CPI YoY exceeds 4% for two consecutive quarters without corresponding FEDFUNDS response.
